data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the legacy project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project file to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Manual `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`** — SDK-style handles these implicitly
3. **Explicit `Configuration` and `Platform` properties** — SDK-style provides sensible defaults
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, and `Deterministic`** — all defaults in SDK-style
6. **Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` conditions** — SDK-style provides sensible defaults for these
7. **Explicit `<Reference>` for framework assemblies** (System, System.Core, etc.) — implicit in SDK-style
8. **Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items** — SDK-style uses file globbing automatically
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — no longer needed since SDK-style generates assembly attributes automatically
10. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — I'll convert this to `TargetFramework` with `net472` to keep the same framework compatibility while modernizing the format, though I could target a newer .NET version if needed.

The user specifically asked to modernize the project file format to SDK-style, so I'll focus on that conversion while preserving `net472`. For the AssemblyInfo.cs file, since SDK-style projects auto-generate assembly attributes, I can move the meaningful metadata like Title, Description, and Company into the project file properties and remove the separate file.

Now I'll create the modernized SDK-style project file.

</Project>

data.content:
Here's what was modernized — **47 lines → 15 lines**:

| Legacy Pattern | What Changed |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion`, `xmlns` on `<Project>` | Replaced with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK |
| `Configuration`, `Platform` defaults | SDK provides these automatically |
| `ProjectGuid` | Not needed in SDK-style |
| `OutputType` Library | Library is the SDK default |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK defaults handle these |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | SDK provides sensible defaults for `Optimize`, `DebugType`, `DefineConstants`, `OutputPath` |
| Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit with SDK-style targeting |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK uses automatic file globbing |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted — attributes moved to project properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Version`, etc.) which SDK auto-generates |
